==2011==
The ceremony took place on 29 September 2011.

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|-
| Biology
| Darryl Gwynne and David Rentz
| "for discovering that a [[Julodimorpha|certain kind of beetle]] mates with a certain kind of Australian [[Stubby bottle|beer bottle]]"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Gwynne | first1=D. T. | last2=Rentz | first2=D. C. F. | title=Beetles On The Bottle: Male Buprestids Mistake Stubbies For Females (Coleoptera) | journal=Australian Journal of Entomology | volume=22 | issue=1 | date=1983 | issn=1326-6756 | doi=10.1111/j.1440-6055.1983.tb01846.x | pages=79–80 | bibcode=1983AuJE...22...79G | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1440-6055.1983.tb01846.x | access-date=2025-12-20| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Chemistry 
| Makoto Imai, Naoki Urushihata, Hideki Tanemura, Yukinobu Tajima, Hideaki Goto, Koichiro Mizoguchi and Junichi Murakami
| "for determining the ideal density of airborne [[wasabi]] (pungent horseradish) to awaken sleeping people in case of a fire or other emergency, and for applying this knowledge to invent the wasabi alarm"
| <ref>{{cite web | title=Odor generation alarm and method for informing unusual situation | website=Google Patents | date=2009-02-05 | url=https://patents.google.com/patent/US9082274B2/ | access-date=2025-12-20}}</ref>
|-
| Literature
| [[John Perry (philosopher)|John Perry]]
| "for his ''Theory of Structured Procrastination'', which says: To be a high achiever, always work on something important, using it as a way to avoid doing something that's even more important"
| <ref>{{cite web | last=Perry | first=John | title=How to Procrastinate and Still Get Things Done | website=The Chronicle of Higher Education | date=1996-02-23 | url=https://www.chronicle.com/article/how-to-procrastinate-and-still-get-things-done/ | access-date=2025-12-20 | url-access=registration | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201112035921/https://www.chronicle.com/article/how-to-procrastinate-and-still-get-things-done/ | archive-date=2020-11-12}}</ref>
|-
| rowspan="6" | Mathematics
| [[Dorothy Martin (spiritualist)|Dorothy Martin]] (who predicted the world would end in 1954)
| rowspan="6" | "for teaching the world to be careful when [[List of dates predicted for apocalyptic events|making mathematical assumptions and calculations]]"
| <ref>{{cite web | last=Moser | first=Whet | title=Apocalypse Oak Park: Dorothy Martin, the Chicagoan Who Predicted the End of the World and Inspired the Theory of Cognitive Dissonance | website=Chicago Magazine | date=2011-05-20 | url=https://www.chicagomag.com/city-life/may-2011/dorothy-martin-the-chicagoan-who-predicted-the-end-of-the-world-and-inspired-the-theory-of-cognitive-dissonance/ | access-date=2025-12-20}}</ref>
|-
| [[Pat Robertson]] (who predicted the world would end in 1982)
| <ref>{{cite web |last1=Peter |first1=Josh |title=Televangelist Pat Robertson predicts Trump win, then chaos, then the end of the world |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/2020/10/20/televangelist-pat-robertson-predicts-trump-win-end-world/5996435002/ |website=[[USA Today]] |date=20 October 2020 |access-date=1 April 2026}}</ref>

| [[Elizabeth Clare Prophet]] (who predicted the world would end in 1990)

|-
| [[Lee Jang Rim]] (who predicted the world would end in 1992)

|-
| [[Harold Camping]] (who predicted the world would end on September 6, 1994 and later predicted that the world will end on October 21, 2011)
| <ref>{{cite web | title=We are almost there! | website=Family Radio | date=2009-10-17 | url=http://www.familyradio.com/graphical/literature/waat/waat_temp.html | access-date=2025-12-20 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111102194628/http://www.familyradio.com/graphical/literature/waat/waat_temp.html | archive-date=2011-11-02}}</ref>{{efn|[[Harold Camping]] later admitted his predictions were wrong and that he regrets his misdeeds.<ref>{{cite web | last=Menzie | first=Nicola | title=Family Radio Founder Harold Camping Repents, Apologizes for False Teachings | website=Christian Post | date=2011-10-30 | url=https://www.christianpost.com/news/family-radio-founder-harold-camping-repents-apologizes-for-false-teachings.html | access-date=2025-12-20}}</ref>}}
|-
| rowspan="2" | Medicine
| Mirjam Tuk, Debra Trampe, and Luk Warlop 
| rowspan="2" | "for demonstrating that people make better decisions about some kinds of things — but worse decisions about other kinds of things, when they have a strong urge to urinate"
| <ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Tuk |first1=M. A. |last2=Trampe |first2=D. |last3=Warlop |first3=L. |year=2011 |title=Inhibitory Spillover: Increased Urination Urgency Facilitates Impulse Control in Unrelated Domains |url=https://spiral.imperial.ac.uk/bitstream/10044/1/10464/2/Tuk_etal_PsychologicalScience_2011.pdf |journal=Psychological Science |volume=22 |issue=5 |pages=627–633 |doi=10.1177/0956797611404901 |pmid=21467548 |s2cid=13896846 |hdl-access=free |hdl=10044/1/10464|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170706045822/https://spiral.imperial.ac.uk/bitstream/10044/1/10464/2/Tuk_etal_PsychologicalScience_2011.pdf |archive-date=6 July 2017 }}</ref>
|- 
| Matthew Lewis, Peter Snyder and Robert Feldman, Robert Pietrzak, David Darby, and Paul Maruff
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Lewis | first1=M.S. | last2=Snyder | first2=P.J. | last3=Pietrzak | first3=R.H. | last4=Darby | first4=D. | last5=Feldman | first5=R.A. | last6=Maruff | first6=P. | title=The effect of acute increase in urge to void on cognitive function in healthy adults | journal=Neurourology and Urodynamics | volume=30 | issue=1 | date=2011 | issn=0733-2467 | pmid=21058363 | s2cid=28730291 | doi=10.1002/nau.20963 | pages=183–187 | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/nau.20963 | access-date=2025-12-20| url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Peace
| [[Arturas Zuokas]], the mayor of [[Vilnius, Lithuania]] 
| "for demonstrating that the problem of illegally parked [[luxury car]]s can be solved by running them over with an [[armored tank]]"
| <ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.vilnius.lt/newvilniusweb/index.php/116/?itemID=94256 |title=Vilnius Mayor Fights Illegally Parked Cars with Tank | website=Vilnius City Municipality| access-date=20 December 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110917191201/http://www.vilnius.lt/newvilniusweb/index.php/116/?itemID=94256  |archive-date=17 September 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title=Vilnius Mayor A.Zuokas Fights Illegally Parked Cars with Tank | website=YouTube | date=2024-03-06 | url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V-fWN0FmcIU | access-date=2025-12-20}}</ref><!--direct video reference from improbable research page-->{{efn|Note, the vehicle used was not a tank, but a [[BTR-60]] [[Armoured personnel carrier]].{{citation needed|date=December 2025}}}}

| Public safety
| [[John Senders]]
| "for conducting a series of safety experiments in which a person drives an automobile on a major highway while a visor repeatedly flaps down over his face, blinding him"

==2012==
The ceremony took place on 20 September 2012.

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"

! scope="col"| Refs
|-
| Acoustics
| Kazutaka Kurihara and Koji Tsukada 
| "for creating the SpeechJammer — a machine that disrupts a person's speech, by making them [[delayed auditory feedback|hear their own spoken words at a very slight delay]]"
| <ref>{{cite arXiv |last1=Kurihara |first1=Kazutaka |last2=Tsukada |first2=Koji |eprint=1202.6106 | title=SpeechJammer: A System Utilizing Artificial Speech Disturbance with Delayed Auditory Feedback | year=2012 | class=cs.HC }}</ref>

==2013==
The ceremony took place on 12 September 2013.

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|-
| Archaeology
| Brian Crandall and Peter Stahl
| "for parboiling a dead shrew, and then swallowing the shrew without chewing, and then carefully examining everything excreted during subsequent days — all so they could see which bones would dissolve inside the human digestive system, and which bones would not"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Crandall | first1=Brian D. | last2=Stahl | first2=Peter W. | title=Human digestive effects on a micromammalian skeleton | journal=Journal of Archaeological Science | volume=22 | issue=6 | date=1995 | doi=10.1016/0305-4403(95)90008-X | pages=789–797 | bibcode=1995JArSc..22..789C | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/030544039590008X | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Biology and Astronomy
| [[Marie Dacke]], Emily Baird, [[Marcus Byrne]], Clarke Scholtz, and Eric J. Warrant 
| "for discovering that when [[Scarabaeus satyrus|dung beetles]]<!--The particular species in the paper--> get lost, they can [[Animal navigation|navigate]] their way home by looking at the [[Milky Way]]"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Dacke | first1=Marie | last2=Baird | first2=Emily | last3=Byrne | first3=Marcus | last4=Scholtz | first4=Clarke H. | last5=Warrant | first5=Eric J. | title=Dung Beetles Use the Milky Way for Orientation | journal=Current Biology | volume=23 | issue=4 | date=2013 | pmid= 23352694 | doi=10.1016/j.cub.2012.12.034 | pages=298–300 | bibcode=2013CBio...23..298D | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0960982212015072 | access-date=2025-12-15}}</ref>
|-
| Chemistry
| Shinsuke Imai, Nobuaki Tsuge, Muneaki Tomotake, Yoshiaki Nagatome, H. Sawada, Toshiyuki Nagata, and Hidehiko Kumagai
| "for discovering that the biochemical process by which onions [[onion tears|make people cry]] is even more complicated than scientists previously realized"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Imai | first1=S. | last2=Tsuge | first2=N. | last3=Tomotake | first3=M. | last4=Nagatome | first4=Y. | last5=Sawada | first5=H. | last6=Nagata | first6=T. | last7=Kumagai | first7=H. | title=An onion enzyme that makes the eyes water | journal=Nature | volume=419 | issue=6908 | date=2002-10-17 | issn=0028-0836 | pmid= 12384686 | s2cid=4407001 | doi=10.1038/419685a | page=685 | url=https://www.nature.com/articles/419685a | access-date=2025-12-15}}</ref>
|-
| Medicine
| Masateru Uchiyama, Xiangyuan Jin, Qi Zhang, Toshihito Hirai, Atsushi Amano, Hisashi Bashuda and Masanori Niimi
| "for assessing the effect of listening to opera, on heart transplant patients who are mice"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Uchiyama | first1=Masateru | last2=Jin | first2=Xiangyuan | last3=Zhang | first3=Qi | last4=Hirai | first4=Toshihito | last5=Amano | first5=Atsushi | last6=Bashuda | first6=Hisashi | last7=Niimi | first7=Masanori | title=Auditory stimulation of opera music induced prolongation of murine cardiac allograft survival and maintained generation of regulatory CD4+CD25+ cells | journal=Journal of Cardiothoracic Surgery | volume=7 | issue=1 | date=2012 | issn=1749-8090 | pmid=22445281 | pmc=3338095 | doi=10.1186/1749-8090-7-26 | doi-access=free | article-number=26 }}</ref>
|-
| rowspan="2" | Peace
| [[Alexander Lukashenko]], [[president of Belarus]]
| "for [[2011 Belarusian protests|making it illegal to applaud in public]]"
| <ref>{{cite news | title=No applause, please | newspaper=The Economist | date=2011-07-07 | url=https://www.economist.com/europe/2011/07/07/no-applause-please | access-date=2025-12-15 | archive-url=https://archive.today/20220907222858/https://www.economist.com/europe/2011/07/07/no-applause-please | archive-date=2022-09-07}}</ref>

| [[Law enforcement in Belarus|Belarus State Police]]
| "for arresting a one-armed man for applauding"
| <ref>{{cite web | author=The Christian Science Monitor | title=In Belarus, one-armed man arrested for clapping | website=The Christian Science Monitor | date=2011-07-08 | url=https://www.csmonitor.com/World/Europe/2011/0708/In-Belarus-one-armed-man-arrested-for-clapping | access-date=2025-12-15}}</ref>
|-
| Probability
| Bert Tolkamp, Marie Haskell, Fritha Langford, David Roberts, and Colin Morgan
| "for making two related discoveries: First, that the longer a cow has been lying down, the more likely that cow will soon stand up; and Second, that once a cow stands up, you cannot easily predict how soon that cow will lie down again"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Tolkamp | first1=Bert J. | last2=Haskell | first2=Marie J. | last3=Langford | first3=Fritha M. | last4=Roberts | first4=David J. | last5=Morgan | first5=Colin A. | title=Are cows more likely to lie down the longer they stand? | journal=Applied Animal Behaviour Science | volume=124 | issue=1–2 | date=2010 | doi=10.1016/j.applanim.2010.02.004 | pages=1–10 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0168159110000547 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Physics
| Alberto Minetti, Yuri Ivanenko, Germana Cappellini, Nadia Dominici, and [[Francesco Lacquaniti]]
| "for discovering that some people would be physically capable of running across the surface of a pond — if those people and that pond were on the moon"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Minetti | first1=Alberto E. | last2=Ivanenko | first2=Yuri P. | last3=Cappellini | first3=Germana | last4=Dominici | first4=Nadia | last5=Lacquaniti | first5=Francesco | title=Humans Running in Place on Water at Simulated Reduced Gravity | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=7 | issue=7 | date=2012-07-18 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=22815681 | pmc=3399875 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0037300 | doi-access=free | article-number=e37300 | bibcode=2012PLoSO...737300M }}</ref>
|-
| Psychology
| Laurent Bègue, [[Brad Bushman]], Oulmann Zerhouni, Baptiste Subra, and Medhi Ourabah
| "for confirming, by experiment, that people who think they are drunk also think they are attractive"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Bègue | first1=Laurent | last2=Bushman | first2=Brad J. | last3=Zerhouni | first3=Oulmann | last4=Subra | first4=Baptiste | last5=Ourabah | first5=Medhi | title='Beauty is in the eye of the beer holder': People who think they are drunk also think they are attractive | journal=British Journal of Psychology | volume=104 | issue=2 | date=2013 | issn=0007-1269 | pmid= 23560668 | s2cid=8354214 | doi=10.1111/j.2044-8295.2012.02114.x | pages=225–234 | url=https://bpspsychub.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.2044-8295.2012.02114.x | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Public Health
| Kasian Bhanganada, Tu Chayavatana, Chumporn Pongnumkul, Anunt Tonmukayakul, Piyasakol Sakolsatayadorn, Krit Komaratal, and Henry Wilde
| "for the medical techniques described in their report ''Surgical Management of an Epidemic of Penile Amputations in Siam'' — techniques which they recommend, except in cases where the amputated penis had been partially eaten by a duck"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Bhanganada | first1=Kasian | last2=Chayavatana | first2=Tu | last3=Pongnumkul | first3=Chumporn | last4=Tonmukayakul | first4=Anunt | last5=Sakolsatayadorn | first5=Piyasakol | last6=Komaratat | first6=Krit | last7=Wilde | first7=Henry | title=Surgical management of an epidemic of penile amputations in siam | journal=The American Journal of Surgery | volume=146 | issue=3 | date=1983 | pmid= 6614331 | doi=10.1016/0002-9610(83)90420-8 | pages=376–382 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/0002961083904208 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Safety Engineering
| Gustano Pizzo{{dagger|sup=yes}}
| "for inventing an electro-mechanical system to trap [[airplane hijacking|airplane hijackers]] — the system drops a hijacker through trap doors, seals him into a package, then drops the encapsulated hijacker through the airplane's specially-installed bomb bay doors, whence he parachutes to earth, where police, having been alerted by radio, await his arrival" 
| <ref>{{cite web | title=Anti hijacking system for aircraft | website=Google Patents | date=1972-11-02 | url=https://patents.google.com/patent/US3811643A | access-date=2025-12-15}}</ref>{{efn|name="ZoltanEgeresi"}}
|}

==2014==
The ceremony took place on 18 September 2014.

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|-
| Arctic Science
| Eigil Reimers and Sindre Eftestøl
| "for testing how reindeer react to seeing humans who are disguised as polar bears"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Reimers | first1=Eigil | last2=Eftestøl | first2=Sindre | title=Response Behaviors of Svalbard Reindeer TOwards Humans and Humans Disguised as Polar Bears on Edgeøya | journal=Arctic, Antarctic, and Alpine Research | volume=44 | issue=4 | date=2012 | issn=1523-0430 | s2cid=130355679 | doi=10.1657/1938-4246-44.4.483 | pages=483–489 | bibcode=2012AAAR...44..483R | url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1657/1938-4246-44.4.483 | access-date=2025-12-15}}</ref>
|-
| Art
| Marina de Tommaso, Michele Sardaro, and Paolo Livrea
| "for measuring the relative pain people suffer while looking at an ugly painting, rather than a pretty painting, while being shot [in the hand] by a powerful laser beam"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Tommaso | first1=Marina de | last2=Sardaro | first2=Michele | last3=Livrea | first3=Paolo | title=Aesthetic value of paintings affects pain thresholds | journal=Consciousness and Cognition | volume=17 | issue=4 | date=2008 | pmid=18762434 | s2cid=1202657 | doi=10.1016/j.concog.2008.07.002 | pages=1152–1162 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S1053810008001177 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Biology
| Vlastimil Hart, Petra Nováková, [[Erich Pascal Malkemper]], Sabine Begall, Vladimír Hanzal, Miloš Ježek, Tomáš Kušta, Veronika Němcová, Jana Adámková, Kateřina Benediktová, Jaroslav Červený and Hynek Burda
| "for carefully documenting that when dogs defecate and urinate, they prefer to align their body axis with [[geomagnetic field|Earth's north-south geomagnetic field lines]]"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Hart | first1=Vlastimil | last2=Nováková | first2=Petra | last3=Malkemper | first3=Erich Pascal | last4=Begall | first4=Sabine | last5=Hanzal | first5=Vladimír | last6=Ježek | first6=Miloš | last7=Kušta | first7=Tomáš | last8=Němcová | first8=Veronika | last9=Adámková | first9=Jana | last10=Benediktová | first10=Kateřina | last11=Červený | first11=Jaroslav | last12=Burda | first12=Hynek | title=Dogs are sensitive to small variations of the Earth's magnetic field | journal=Frontiers in Zoology | volume=10 | issue=1 | date=2013 | issn=1742-9994 | pmid=24370002 | pmc=3882779 | doi=10.1186/1742-9994-10-80 | doi-access=free | article-number=80 }}</ref>
|-
| Economics
| [[Italian National Institute of Statistics]]
| "for proudly taking the lead in fulfilling the [[European Union]] mandate for each country to increase the official size of its national economy by including revenues from prostitution, illegal drug sales, smuggling, and all other unlawful financial transactions between willing participants"
| <ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.istat.it/notizia/sec-2010|title=Notizia - Sec 2010|publisher=ISTAT |date=28 January 2014 |access-date=15 December 2025|quote=Tuttavia una delle riserve trasversali ha una rilevanza maggiore e riguarda l'inserimento nei conti delle attività illegali, in ottemperanza al principio di esaustività, già introdotto dal Sec 95: le stime devono dunque comprendere tutte le attività che producono reddito, indipendentemente dal loro status giuridico. Le attività illegali di cui tutti i paesi devono inserire una stima nei conti (e quindi nel Pil) sono: traffico di sostanze stupefacenti, servizi della prostituzione e contrabbando (di sigarette o alcol). La metodologia di stima della dimensione economica di tali attività è coerente con le linee guida stabilite da Eurostat.|language=it}}</ref>
|-
| Medicine
| Ian Humphreys, Sonal Saraiya, Walter Belenky and James Dworkin
| "for treating "uncontrollable" nosebleeds, using the method of nasal-packing-with-strips-of-cured-pork."
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Humphreys | first1=Ian | last2=Saraiya | first2=Sonal | last3=Belenky | first3=Walter | last4=Dworkin | first4=James | title=Nasal Packing with Strips of Cured Pork as Treatment for Uncontrollable Epistaxis in a Patient with Glanzmann Thrombasthenia | journal=Annals of Otology, Rhinology & Laryngology | volume=120 | issue=11 | date=2011 | issn=0003-4894 | pmid=22224315 | s2cid=37412350 | doi=10.1177/000348941112001107 | pages=732–736 | url=https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/000348941112001107 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Neuroscience
| Jiangang Liu, Jun Li, Lu Feng, Ling Li, Jie Tian, and Kang Lee
| "for trying to understand what happens in the brains of people who [[Perceptions of religious imagery in natural phenomena|see the face of Jesus in a piece of toast]]"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Liu | first1=Jiangang | last2=Li | first2=Jun | last3=Feng | first3=Lu | last4=Li | first4=Ling | last5=Tian | first5=Jie | last6=Lee | first6=Kang | title=Seeing Jesus in toast: Neural and behavioral correlates of face pareidolia | journal=Cortex | volume=53 | date=2014 | pmid=24583223 | pmc=3980010 | doi=10.1016/j.cortex.2014.01.013 | pages=60–77 }}</ref>
|-
| Nutrition
| Raquel Rubio, Anna Jofré, Belén Martín, Teresa Aymerich, and Margarita Garriga
| "for their study titled ''Characterization of Lactic Acid Bacteria Isolated from Infant Faeces as Potential Probiotic Starter Cultures for Fermented Sausages''"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Rubio | first1=Raquel | last2=Jofré | first2=Anna | last3=Martín | first3=Belén | last4=Aymerich | first4=Teresa | last5=Garriga | first5=Margarita | title=Characterization of lactic acid bacteria isolated from infant faeces as potential probiotic starter cultures for fermented sausages | journal=Food Microbiology | volume=38 | date=2014 | pmid=24290655 | doi=10.1016/j.fm.2013.07.015 | pages=303–311 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0740002013001548 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Physics
| Kiyoshi Mabuchi, Kensei Tanaka, Daichi Uchijima and Rina Sakai
| "for measuring the [[coefficient of friction|amount of friction]] between a shoe and a [[banana skin]], and between a banana skin and the floor, when a person steps on a banana skin that's on the floor"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Mabuchi | first1=Kiyoshi | last2=Tanaka | first2=Kensei | last3=Uchijima | first3=Daichi | last4=Sakai | first4=Rina | title=Frictional Coefficient under Banana Skin | journal=Tribology Online | volume=7 | issue=3 | date=2012 | issn=1881-2198 | doi=10.2474/trol.7.147 | pages=147–151 | url=https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/trol/7/3/7_147/_article | access-date=2025-12-15| doi-access=free }}</ref>
|-
| Psychology
| Peter K. Jonason, Amy Jones, and Minna Lyons
| "for amassing evidence that people who habitually stay up late are, on average, [[dark triad|more self-admiring, more manipulative, and more psychopathic]] than people who habitually arise early in the morning"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Jonason | first1=Peter K. | last2=Jones | first2=Amy | last3=Lyons | first3=Minna | title=Creatures of the night: Chronotypes and the Dark Triad traits | journal=Personality and Individual Differences | volume=55 | issue=5 | date=2013 | s2cid=17020467 | doi=10.1016/j.paid.2013.05.001 | pages=538–541 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0191886913001918 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| rowspan="2" | Public Health
| [[Jaroslav Flegr]], Jan Havlíček and Jitka Hanušova-Lindova 
| rowspan="2" | "for investigating whether it is mentally hazardous for a human being to own a cat"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Flegr | first1=J. | last2=Havlícek | first2=J. | title=Changes in the personality profile of young women with latent toxoplasmosis | journal=Folia Parasitologica | volume=46 | issue=1 | date=1999 | issn=0015-5683 | pmid=10353191 | pages=22–28 }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Flegr | first1=Jaroslav | last2=Preiss | first2=Marek | last3=Klose | first3=Jiřı́ | last4=Havlı́ček | first4=Jan | last5=Vitáková | first5=Martina | last6=Kodym | first6=Petr | title=Decreased level of psychobiological factor novelty seeking and lower intelligence in men latently infected with the protozoan parasite Toxoplasma gondii Dopamine, a missing link between schizophrenia and toxoplasmosis? | journal=Biological Psychology | volume=63 | issue=3 | date=2003 | doi=10.1016/S0301-0511(03)00075-9 | pages=253–268 | pmid=12853170 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0301051103000759 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| David Hanauer, Naren Ramakrishnan, Lisa Seyfried
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Hanauer | first1=David A. | last2=Ramakrishnan | first2=Naren | last3=Seyfried | first3=Lisa S. | title=Describing the Relationship between Cat Bites and Human Depression Using Data from an Electronic Health Record | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=8 | issue=8 | date=2013-08-01 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=23936453 | pmc=3731284 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0070585 | doi-access=free | article-number=e70585 | bibcode=2013PLoSO...870585H }}</ref>
|}

==2015 ==
The 25th First Annual Ig Nobel Prize Ceremony took place on 17 September 2015 and was held at the [[Memorial Hall (Harvard University)#Sanders Theatre|Harvard's Sanders Theatre]].

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Biology
| Bruno Grossi, Omar Larach, Mauricio Canals, Rodrigo A. Vásquez, José Iriarte-Díaz
| "for observing that when you attach a weighted stick to the rear end of a chicken, the chicken then walks in a manner similar to that in which dinosaurs are thought to have walked"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Grossi | first1=Bruno | last2=Iriarte-Díaz | first2=José | last3=Larach | first3=Omar | last4=Canals | first4=Mauricio | last5=Vásquez | first5=Rodrigo A. | title=Walking Like Dinosaurs: Chickens with Artificial Tails Provide Clues about Non-Avian Theropod Locomotion | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=9 | issue=2 | date=2014-02-05 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=24505491 | pmc=3915051 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0088458 | doi-access=free | article-number=e88458 | bibcode=2014PLoSO...988458G }}</ref>
|-
| Chemistry
| Callum Ormonde, [[Colin Raston]], Tom Yuan, Stephan Kudlacek, Sameeran Kunche, Joshua N. Smith, William A. Brown, Kaitlin Pugliese, Tivoli Olsen, Mariam Iftikhar, and Gregory Weiss
| "for inventing a chemical recipe to partially un-boil an egg"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Yuan | first1=Tom Z. | last2=Ormonde | first2=Callum F. G. | last3=Kudlacek | first3=Stephan T. | last4=Kunche | first4=Sameeran | last5=Smith | first5=Joshua N. | last6=Brown | first6=William A. | last7=Pugliese | first7=Kaitlin M. | last8=Olsen | first8=Tivoli J. | last9=Iftikhar | first9=Mariam | last10=Raston | first10=Colin L. | last11=Weiss | first11=Gregory A. | title=Shear-Stress-Mediated Refolding of Proteins from Aggregates and Inclusion Bodies | journal=ChemBioChem | volume=16 | issue=3 | date=2015-02-09 | issn=1439-4227 | pmid=25620679 | pmc=4388321 | doi=10.1002/cbic.201402427 | pages=393–396 }}</ref>
|-
| Diagnostic Medicine
| Diallah Karim, Anthony Harnden, Nigel D'Souza, Andrew Huang, Abdel Kader Allouni, Helen Ashdown, Richard J. Stevens, and Simon Kreckler
| "for determining that [[acute appendicitis]] can be accurately diagnosed by the amount of pain evident when the patient is driven over [[speed bump]]s"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Ashdown | first1=H. F. | last2=D'Souza | first2=N. | last3=Karim | first3=D. | last4=Stevens | first4=R. J. | last5=Huang | first5=A. | last6=Harnden | first6=A. | title=Pain over speed bumps in diagnosis of acute appendicitis: diagnostic accuracy study | journal=BMJ | volume=345 | issue=dec14 14 | date=2012-12-14 | issn=1756-1833 | pmid=23247977 | pmc=3524367 | doi=10.1136/bmj.e8012 | article-number=e8012 }}</ref>
|-
| Economics
| The [[Metropolitan Police Bureau|Bangkok Metropolitan Police]]
| "for offering to pay policemen extra cash if the policemen refuse to take bribes"
| <ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.khaosodenglish.com/detail.php?newsid=1442571460|title=Thailand Wins 'Ig Nobel' Distinction for Bribing Cops not to Take Bribes|date=18 September 2015|publisher=Khaosod English|access-date=27 October 2015|archive-date=6 May 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160506083959/http://www.khaosodenglish.com/detail.php?newsid=1442571460}}</ref>
|-
| Literature
| [[Mark Dingemanse]], Francisco Torreira, and Nick J. Enfield
| "for discovering that the word ''huh?'' (or its equivalent) seems to exist in every human language — and for not being completely sure why"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Dingemanse | first1=Mark | last2=Torreira | first2=Francisco | last3=Enfield | first3=N. J. | title=Is "Huh?" a Universal Word? Conversational Infrastructure and the Convergent Evolution of Linguistic Items | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=8 | issue=11 | date=2013-11-08 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=24260108 | pmc=3832628 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0078273 | doi-access=free | article-number=e78273 | bibcode=2013PLoSO...878273D }}</ref>
|-
| Management
| Gennaro Bernile, Vineet Bhagwat, and [[Raghavendra Rau|P. Raghavendra Rau]]
| "for discovering that many business leaders developed during childhood a fondness for risk-taking, when they experienced natural disasters (such as earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, tsunamis, and wildfires) that — for them — had no dire personal consequences"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Bernile | first1=Gennaro | last2=Bhagwat | first2=Vineet | last3=Rau | first3=P. Raghavendra | title=What Doesn't Kill You Will Only Make You More Risk-Loving: Early-Life Disasters and CEO Behavior | journal=The Journal of Finance | volume=72 | issue=1 | date=2017 | issn=0022-1082 | s2cid=219339281 | doi=10.1111/jofi.12432 | pages=167–206 | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jofi.12432 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Mathematics
| Elisabeth Oberzaucher and Karl Grammer
| "for trying to use mathematical techniques to determine whether and how [[Ismail Ibn Sharif|Moulay Ismael the Bloodthirsty]], the [[Alawi Sultanate|Sharifian Emperor of Morocco]], managed,  during the years from 1697 through 1727, to father 888 children."
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Oberzaucher | first1=Elisabeth | last2=Grammer | first2=Karl | title=The Case of Moulay Ismael - Fact or Fancy? | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=9 | issue=2 | date=2014-02-14 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=24551034 | pmc=3925083 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0085292 | doi-access=free | article-number=e85292 | bibcode=2014PLoSO...985292O }}</ref>
|-
| rowspan="2" | Medicine
| Hajime Kimata
| rowspan="2" | "for experiments to study the biomedical benefits or biomedical consequences of intense kissing (and other intimate, interpersonal activities)"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last=Kimata | first=Hajime | title=Kissing reduces allergic skin wheal responses and plasma neurotrophin levels | journal=Physiology & Behavior | volume=80 | issue=2–3 | date=2003 | pmid=14637240 | s2cid=13122664 | doi=10.1016/j.physbeh.2003.09.004 | pages=395–398 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0031938403002956 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last=Kimata | first=Hajime | title=Reduction of allergic skin weal responses by sexual intercourse in allergic patients | journal=Sexual and Relationship Therapy | volume=19 | issue=2 | date=2004 | issn=1468-1994 | s2cid=145394223 | doi=10.1080/14681990410001691361 | pages=151–154 | url=http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/14681990410001691361 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref name="m164">{{cite journal | last=Kimata | first=H. | title=Kissing selectively decreases allergen-specific IgE production in atopic patients | journal=Journal of Psychosomatic Research | volume=60 | issue=5 | date=2006 | pmid=16650596 | doi=10.1016/j.jpsychores.2005.09.007 | pages=545–547 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0022399905004253 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Jaroslava Durdiaková, Peter Celec, Natália Kamodyová, Tatiana Sedláčková, Gabriela Repiská, Barbara Sviežená, and Gabriel Minárik
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Kamodyová | first1=Natália | last2=Durdiaková | first2=Jaroslava | last3=Celec | first3=Peter | last4=Sedláčková | first4=Tatiana | last5=Repiská | first5=Gabriela | last6=Sviežená | first6=Barbara | last7=Minárik | first7=Gabriel | title=Prevalence and persistence of male DNA identified in mixed saliva samples after intense kissing | journal=Forensic Science International: Genetics | volume=7 | issue=1 | date=2013 | pmid=22917815 | doi=10.1016/j.fsigen.2012.07.007 | pages=124–128 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S1872497312001834 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Physics
| Patricia Yang, [[David Hu (scientist)|David Hu]], and Jonathan Pham, Jerome Choo
| "for testing the biological principle that nearly all mammals [[Urination|empty their bladders]] in about 21 seconds (plus or minus 13 seconds)"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Yang | first1=Patricia J. | last2=Pham | first2=Jonathan | last3=Choo | first3=Jerome | last4=Hu | first4=David L. | title=Duration of urination does not change with body size | journal=Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences | volume=111 | issue=33 | date=2014-08-19 | issn=0027-8424 | pmid=24969420 | pmc=4143032 | doi=10.1073/pnas.1402289111 | pages=11932–11937 | doi-access=free | bibcode=2014PNAS..11111932Y }}</ref>
|-
| rowspan="2" | Physiology and Entomology
| [[Justin O. Schmidt|Justin Schmidt]]
| "for painstakingly creating the [[Schmidt Sting Pain Index]], which rates the relative pain people feel when stung by various insects"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Schmidt | first1=Justin O. | last2=Blum | first2=Murray S. | last3=Overal | first3=William L. | title=Hemolytic activities of stinging insect venoms | journal=Archives of Insect Biochemistry and Physiology | volume=1 | issue=2 | date=1983 | issn=0739-4462 | doi=10.1002/arch.940010205 | pages=155–160 | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/arch.940010205 | access-date=2025-12-15| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Michael L. Smith
| "for carefully arranging for [[honey bee]]s to sting him repeatedly on 25 different locations on his body, to learn which locations are the least painful (the skull, middle toe tip, and upper arm), and which are the most painful (the nostril, upper lip, and penis shaft)"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last=Smith | first=Michael L. | title=Honey bee sting pain index by body location | journal=PeerJ | volume=2 | date=2014-04-03 | issn=2167-8359 | pmid=24765572 | pmc=3994616 | doi=10.7717/peerj.338 | doi-access=free | article-number=e338}}</ref>
|}

==2016==

The ceremony took place on 22 September 2016.<ref>{{cite web | last=Amos | first=Jonathan | title=Ig Nobel win for Alpine 'goat man' | website=BBC News | date=2016-09-22 | url=https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-37443204 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Reproduction
| [[Ahmed Shafik (sexologist)|Ahmed Shafik]]{{dagger|sup=yes}} 
| "for studying the effects of wearing polyester, cotton, or wool trousers on the sex life of rats, and for conducting similar tests with human males"
|<ref>{{cite journal | last=Shafik | first=Ahmed | title=Effect of Different Types of Textiles on Sexual Activity | journal=European Urology | volume=24 | issue=3 | date=1993 | issn=0302-2838 | pmid=8262106 | doi=10.1159/000474332 | pages=375–380 | url=https://www.karger.com/Article/FullText/474332 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last=Shafik | first=Ahmed | title=Contraceptive efficacy of polyester-induced azoospermia in normal men | journal=Contraception | volume=45 | issue=5 | date=1992 | pmid=1623716 | doi=10.1016/0010-7824(92)90157-O | pages=439–451 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/001078249290157O | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Economics
| Mark Avis, Sarah Forbes, and Shelagh Ferguson
| "for assessing the perceived personalities of rocks, from a sales and marketing perspective"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Avis | first1=Mark | last2=Forbes | first2=Sarah | last3=Ferguson | first3=Shelagh | title=The brand personality of rocks: A critical evaluation of a brand personality scale | journal=Marketing Theory | volume=14 | issue=4 | date=2014 | issn=1470-5931 | s2cid=147602379 | doi=10.1177/1470593113512323 | pages=451–475 | url=https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/1470593113512323 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Physics
| Gábor Horváth, Miklós Blahó, György Kriska, Ramón Hegedüs, Balázs Gerics, Róbert Farkas, Susanne Åkesson, Péter Malik, and Hansruedi Wildermuth
| "for discovering why white-haired horses are the most [[horsefly]]-proof horses, and for discovering why [[dragonfly|dragonflies]] are fatally attracted to black tombstones"
| <ref name="n127">{{cite journal | last1=Horváth | first1=Gábor | last2=Blahó | first2=Miklós | last3=Kriska | first3=György | last4=Hegedüs | first4=Ramón | last5=Gerics | first5=Balázs | last6=Farkas | first6=Róbert | last7=Åkesson | first7=Susanne | title=An unexpected advantage of whiteness in horses: the most horsefly-proof horse has a depolarizing white coat | journal=Proceedings of the Royal Society B: Biological Sciences | volume=277 | issue=1688 | date=2010-06-07 | issn=0962-8452 | pmid=20129982 | pmc=2871857 | doi=10.1098/rspb.2009.2202 | pages=1643–1650 }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Horváth | first1=Gábor | last2=Malik | first2=Péter | last3=Kriska | first3=György | last4=Wildermuth | first4=Hansruedi | title=Ecological traps for dragonflies in a cemetery: the attraction of Sympetrum species (Odonata: Libellulidae) by horizontally polarizing black gravestones | journal=Freshwater Biology | volume=52 | issue=9 | date=2007 | issn=0046-5070 | s2cid=29452669 | doi=10.1111/j.1365-2427.2007.01798.x | pages=1700–1709 | bibcode=2007FrBio..52.1700H | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1365-2427.2007.01798.x | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Chemistry
| [[Volkswagen]]
| "for solving the problem of excessive automobile pollution emissions by automatically, electromechanically producing fewer emissions [[Volkswagen emissions scandal|whenever the cars are being tested]]"
| <ref>{{cite web | title=EPA, California Notify Volkswagen of Clean Air Act Violations / Carmaker allegedly used software that circumvents emissions testing for certain air pollutants | website=19january2017snapshot.epa.gov | date=2016-04-15 | url=https://19january2017snapshot.epa.gov/newsreleases/epa-california-notify-volkswagen-clean-air-act-violations-carmaker-allegedly-used_.html | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Medicine
| [[Christoph Helmchen]], Carina Palzer, Thomas Münte, Silke Anders, and Andreas Sprenger
| "for discovering that if you have an itch on the left side of your body, you can relieve it by looking into a mirror and scratching the right side of your body (and vice versa)."
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Helmchen | first1=Christoph | last2=Palzer | first2=Carina | last3=Münte | first3=Thomas F. | last4=Anders | first4=Silke | last5=Sprenger | first5=Andreas | title=Itch Relief by Mirror Scratching. A Psychophysical Study | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=8 | issue=12 | date=2013-12-26 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=24386113 | pmc=3873464 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0082756 | doi-access=free | article-number=e82756 | bibcode=2013PLoSO...882756H }}</ref>
|-
| Psychology
| Evelyne Debey, Maarten De Schryver, Gordon Logan, Kristina Suchotzki, and Bruno Verschuere
| "for asking a thousand liars how often they lie, and for deciding whether to believe those answers"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Debey | first1=Evelyne | last2=De Schryver | first2=Maarten | last3=Logan | first3=Gordon D. | last4=Suchotzki | first4=Kristina | last5=Verschuere | first5=Bruno | title=From junior to senior Pinocchio: A cross-sectional lifespan investigation of deception | journal=Acta Psychologica | volume=160 | date=2015 | pmid=26182909 | doi=10.1016/j.actpsy.2015.06.007 | pages=58–68 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0001691815300184 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Peace
| [[Gordon Pennycook]], James Allan Cheyne, Nathaniel Barr, Derek Koehler, and Jonathan Fugelsang
| "for their scholarly study called ''On the Reception and Detection of Pseudo-Profound Bullshit''"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Pennycook | first1=Gordon | last2=Allan Cheyne | first2=James | last3=Barr | first3=Nathaniel | last4=Koehler | first4=Derek J. | last5=Fugelsang | first5=Jonathan A. | title=On the reception and detection of pseudo-profound bullshit | journal=Judgment and Decision Making | volume=10 | issue=6 | date=2015 | issn=1930-2975 | s2cid=16505606 | doi=10.1017/S1930297500006999 | pages=549–563 | doi-access=free }}</ref>
|-
| rowspan="2" | Biology
| [[Charles A. Foster|Charles Foster]]
| "for living in the wild as, at different times, a badger, an otter, a deer, a fox, and a bird"
| <ref>{{cite book | last=Foster | first=Charles | title=Being a Beast: Adventures Across the Species Divide | publisher=Serpent's Tail | publication-place=London | date=2016 | isbn=978-1-78125-534-6 | page=}}</ref>
|-
| [[Thomas Thwaites (designer)|Thomas Thwaites]]
| "for creating prosthetic extensions of his limbs that allowed him to move in the manner of, and spend time roaming hills in the company of, goats"
| <ref>{{cite book | last=Thwaites | first=Thomas | title=GoatMan: How I Took a Holiday from Being Human (one man's journey to leave humanity behind and become like a goat) | publisher=Princeton Architectural Press | publication-place=New York | date=2016-05-17 | isbn=978-1-61689-405-4 | page=}}</ref>
|-
| Literature
| {{ill|Fredrik Sjöberg|sv|Fredrik Sjöberg (författare)}}
| "for his three-volume autobiographical work about the pleasures of collecting flies that are dead, and flies that are not yet dead"
| <ref>{{cite book | last=Sjöberg | first=Fredrik | title=The Fly Trap: A Memoir | publisher=Pantheon | date=2015 | isbn=978-1-101-87015-0 | page=}}</ref>
|-
| Perception
| Atsuki Higashiyama and Kohei Adachi
| "for investigating whether things look different when you bend over and view them between your legs"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Higashiyama | first1=Atsuki | last2=Adachi | first2=Kohei | title=Perceived size and perceived distance of targets viewed from between the legs: Evidence for proprioceptive theory | journal=Vision Research | volume=46 | issue=23 | date=2006 | pmid=16979687 | s2cid=18168744 | doi=10.1016/j.visres.2006.04.002 | pages=3961–3976 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0042698906002112 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|}

==2017==
The ceremony took place on 14 September 2017.<ref name="Ig Nobel-2006">{{Cite web |date=1 August 2006 |title=The Ig Nobel Prize Winners |url=https://www.improbable.com/ig-about/winners/}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://cen.acs.org/articles/95/i37/2017-Ig-Nobel-Prizes.html |title=2017 Ig Nobel Prizes {{pipe}} September 18, 2017 Issue – Vol. 95 Issue 37 {{pipe}} Chemical & Engineering News |website=cen.acs.org |date=21 January 2025 }}</ref>

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Physics
| Marc-Antoine Fardin
| "for using fluid dynamics to probe the question ''Can a Cat Be Both a Solid and a Liquid?''"
| <ref>{{Cite journal |last=Fardin |first=Marc-Antoine |date=July 2014 |title=On the Rheology of Cats |url=https://www.rheology.org/SoR/Publications/RheoBulletin/RB2014Jul.pdf |url-status=live |journal=Rheology Bulletin |volume=83 |issue=2 |pages=16–17, 30 |issn=0035-4538 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181220171954/https://www.rheology.org/sor/publications/rheology_b/RB2014Jul.pdf/ |archive-date=20 December 2018}}</ref>
|-
| Peace 
| Milo Puhan, Alex Suarez, Christian Lo Cascio, Alfred Zahn, Markus Heitz, and Otto Braendli
| "for demonstrating that regular playing of a [[didgeridoo]] is an effective treatment for obstructive [[sleep apnoea]] and snoring"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Puhan | first1=Milo A | last2=Suarez | first2=Alex | last3=Cascio | first3=Christian Lo | last4=Zahn | first4=Alfred | last5=Heitz | first5=Markus | last6=Braendli | first6=Otto | title=Didgeridoo playing as alternative treatment for obstructive sleep apnoea syndrome: randomised controlled trial | journal=BMJ | volume=332 | issue=7536 | date=2006-02-04 | issn=0959-8138 | pmid=16377643 | pmc=1360393 | doi=10.1136/bmj.38705.470590.55 | pages=266–270 }}</ref>
|-
| Economics
| Matthew Rockloff and Nancy Greer
| "for their experiments to see how contact with a live crocodile affects a person's willingness to gamble"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Rockloff | first1=Matthew J. | last2=Greer | first2=Nancy | title=Never Smile at a Crocodile: Betting on Electronic Gaming Machines is Intensified by Reptile-Induced Arousal | journal=Journal of Gambling Studies | volume=26 | issue=4 | date=2010 | issn=1050-5350 | pmid=20052606 | s2cid=5354413 | doi=10.1007/s10899-009-9174-4 | pages=571–581 | url=http://link.springer.com/10.1007/s10899-009-9174-4 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Anatomy
| James Heathcote
| "for his medical research study ''Why Do Old Men Have Big Ears?''"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last=Heathcote | first=J. A | title=Why do old men have big ears? | journal=BMJ | volume=311 | issue=7021 | date=1995-12-23 | issn=0959-8138 | pmid=8541753 | pmc=2539087 | doi=10.1136/bmj.311.7021.1668 | page=1668 }}</ref>
|-
| Biology
| Kazunori Yoshizawa, Rodrigo Ferreira, Yoshitaka Kamimura, and Charles Lienhard
| "for their discovery of a female penis, and a male vagina, [[Neotrogla|in a cave insect]]"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Yoshizawa | first1=Kazunori | last2=Ferreira | first2=Rodrigo L. | last3=Kamimura | first3=Yoshitaka | last4=Lienhard | first4=Charles | title=Female Penis, Male Vagina, and Their Correlated Evolution in a Cave Insect | journal=Current Biology | volume=24 | issue=9 | date=2014 | pmid=24746797 | doi=10.1016/j.cub.2014.03.022 | pages=1006–1010 | bibcode=2014CBio...24.1006Y | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0960982214003145 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>{{efn|The short video acceptance speech for the award was filmed in a cave.}}
|-
| Fluid Dynamics
| Jiwon Han
| "for studying the dynamics of liquid-sloshing, to learn what happens when a person walks backwards while carrying a cup of coffee"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last=Han | first=Jiwon | title=A Study on the Coffee Spilling Phenomena in the Low Impulse Regime | journal=Achievements in the Life Sciences | volume=10 | issue=1 | date=2016 | doi=10.1016/j.als.2016.05.009 | pages=87–101 | doi-access=free }}</ref>{{efn|Jiwon Han was a high school student at [[Korean Minjok Leadership Academy]] when he wrote the paper.}}
|-
| Nutrition
| Fernanda Ito, Enrico Bernard, and Rodrigo Torres
| "for the first scientific report of human blood in the diet of the [[hairy-legged vampire bat]]"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Ito | first1=Fernanda | last2=Bernard | first2=Enrico | last3=Torres | first3=Rodrigo A. | title=What is for Dinner? First Report of Human Blood in the Diet of the Hairy-Legged Vampire Bat Diphylla ecaudata | journal=Acta Chiropterologica | volume=18 | issue=2 | date=2016-12-01 | issn=1508-1109 | s2cid=88845021 | doi=10.3161/15081109ACC2016.18.2.017 | page=509 | url=https://bioone.org/journals/acta-chiropterologica/volume-18/issue-2/15081109ACC2016.18.2.017/What-is-for-Dinner-First-Report-of-Human-Blood-in/10.3161/15081109ACC2016.18.2.017.full | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Medicine
| Jean-Pierre Royet, David Meunier, Nicolas Torquet, Anne-Marie Mouly, and Tao Jiang
| "for using advanced brain-scanning technology to measure the extent to which some people are disgusted by cheese"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Royet | first1=Jean-Pierre | last2=Meunier | first2=David | last3=Torquet | first3=Nicolas | last4=Mouly | first4=Anne-Marie | last5=Jiang | first5=Tao | title=The Neural Bases of Disgust for Cheese: An fMRI Study | journal=Frontiers in Human Neuroscience | volume=10 | date=2016-10-17 | issn=1662-5161 | pmid=27799903 | pmc=5065955 | doi=10.3389/fnhum.2016.00511 | doi-access=free | page=511}}</ref>
|-
| Cognition
| Matteo Martini, Ilaria Bufalari, Maria Antonietta Stazi, and Salvatore Maria Aglioti
| "for demonstrating that many identical twins cannot tell themselves apart visually"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Martini | first1=Matteo | last2=Bufalari | first2=Ilaria | last3=Stazi | first3=Maria Antonietta | last4=Aglioti | first4=Salvatore Maria | title=Is That Me or My Twin? Lack of Self-Face Recognition Advantage in Identical Twins | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=10 | issue=4 | date=2015-04-08 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=25853249 | pmc=4390198 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0120900 | doi-access=free | article-number=e0120900 | bibcode=2015PLoSO..1020900M }}</ref>
|-
| Obstetrics
| Marisa López-Teijón, Álex García-Faura, Alberto Prats-Galino, and Luis Pallarés Aniorte
| "for showing that a developing human fetus responds more strongly to music that is played electromechanically inside the mother's vagina than to music that is played electromechanically on the mother's belly"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=López-Teijón | first1=Marisa | last2=García-Faura | first2=Álex | last3=Prats-Galino | first3=Alberto | title=Fetal facial expression in response to intravaginal music emission | journal=Ultrasound | volume=23 | issue=4 | date=2015 | issn=1742-271X | pmc=4616906 | pmid=26539240 | doi=10.1177/1742271X15609367 | pages=216–223 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title=Acoustic Fetal Stimulation Device| website=Google Patents | date=2014-03-26 | url=https://patents.google.com/patent/ES2546919B1/en | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|}

==2018==

The ceremony took place on 13 September 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.improbable.com/ig/2018/|title=Improbable Research|date=6 February 2022}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=2018 Ig Nobel Prizes |url=https://cen.acs.org/people/awards/2018-Ig-Nobel-Prizes/96/i37 |access-date=2024-10-22 |website=Chemical & Engineering News |language=en}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Medicine
| Marc Mitchell and David Wartinger
| "for using roller coaster rides to try to hasten the passage of kidney stones"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Mitchell | first1=Marc A. | last2=Wartinger | first2=David D. | title=Validation of a Functional Pyelocalyceal Renal Model for the Evaluation of Renal Calculi Passage While Riding a Roller Coaster | journal=Journal of Osteopathic Medicine | volume=116 | issue=10 | date=2016-10-01 | issn=2702-3648 |  pmid=27669068 | doi=10.7556/jaoa.2016.128 | pages=647–652 | doi-access=free }}</ref>
|-
| Anthropology
| Tomas Persson, Gabriela-Alina Sauciuc, and Elainie Madsen
| "for collecting evidence, in a zoo, that chimpanzees imitate humans about as often, and about as well, as humans imitate chimpanzees"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Persson | first1=Tomas | last2=Sauciuc | first2=Gabriela-Alina | last3=Madsen | first3=Elainie Alenkær | title=Spontaneous cross-species imitation in interactions between chimpanzees and zoo visitors | journal=Primates | volume=59 | issue=1 | date=2018 | issn=0032-8332 | pmid=28815382 | pmc=5740201 | doi=10.1007/s10329-017-0624-9 | pages=19–29 }}</ref>
|-
| Biology
| Paul Becher, Sebastien Lebreton, Erika Wallin, Erik Hedenstrom, Felipe Borrero-Echeverry, Marie Bengtsson, Volker Jorger, and Peter Witzgall
| "for demonstrating that wine experts can reliably identify, by smell, the presence of a single fly in a glass of wine"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Becher | first1=Paul G. | last2=Lebreton | first2=Sebastien | last3=Wallin | first3=Erika A. | last4=Hedenström | first4=Erik | last5=Borrero | first5=Felipe | last6=Bengtsson | first6=Marie | last7=Joerger | first7=Volker | last8=Witzgall | first8=Peter | title=The Scent of the Fly | journal=Journal of Chemical Ecology | volume=44 | issue=5 | date=2018 | issn=0098-0331 | pmid=29611073 | doi=10.1007/s10886-018-0950-4 | pages=431–435 | bibcode=2018JCEco..44..431B | url=http://link.springer.com/10.1007/s10886-018-0950-4 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref> 
|-
| Chemistry
| Paula Romão, Adília Alarcão and the late César Viana
| "for measuring the degree to which human saliva is a good cleaning agent for dirty surfaces"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Romão | first1=Paula M. S. | last2=Alarcão | first2=Adília M. | last3=Viana | first3=César A. N. | title=Human saliva as a cleaning agent for dirty surfaces | journal=Studies in Conservation | volume=35 | issue=3 | date=1990 | issn=0039-3630 | doi=10.1179/sic.1990.35.3.153 | pages=153–155 | url=http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1179/sic.1990.35.3.153 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Medical Education
| Akira Horiuchi
| "for the medical report ''Colonoscopy in the Sitting Position: Lessons Learned From Self-Colonoscopy''"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Horiuchi | first1=Akira | last2=Nakayama | first2=Yoshiko | title=Colonoscopy in the sitting position: lessons learned from self-colonoscopy by using a small-caliber, variable-stiffness colonoscope | journal=Gastrointestinal Endoscopy | volume=63 | issue=1 | date=2006 | pmid=16377328 | doi=10.1016/j.gie.2005.10.014 | pages=119–120 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0016510705030129 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Literature
| Thea Blackler, Rafael Gomez, Vesna Popovic and M. Helen Thompson
| "for documenting that most people who use complicated products do not read the instruction manual"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Blackler | first1=Alethea L. | last2=Gomez | first2=Rafael | last3=Popovic | first3=Vesna | last4=Thompson | first4=M. Helen | title=Life Is Too Short to RTFM: How Users Relate to Documentation and Excess Features in Consumer Products | journal=Interacting with Computers | volume=28 | issue=1 | date=2016 | issn=0953-5438 | doi=10.1093/iwc/iwu023 | pages=27–46 | url=https://academic.oup.com/iwc/article-lookup/doi/10.1093/iwc/iwu023 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Nutrition
| James Cole
| "for calculating that the caloric intake from a human-cannibalism diet is significantly lower than the caloric intake from most other traditional meat diets"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last=Cole | first=James | title=Assessing the calorific significance of episodes of human cannibalism in the Palaeolithic | journal=Scientific Reports | volume=7 | issue=1 | date=2017-04-06 | issn=2045-2322 | pmid=28383521 | pmc=5382840 | doi=10.1038/srep44707 | article-number=44707 | bibcode=2017NatSR...744707C }}</ref>
|-
| Peace 
| Francisco Alonso, Cristina Esteban, Andrea Serge, Maria-Luisa Ballestar, Jaime Sanmartín, Constanza Calatayud, and Beatriz Alamar
| "for measuring the frequency, motivation, and effects of shouting and cursing while driving an automobile"
| {{citation needed|date=December 2025}}
|-
| Reproductive Medicine
| John Barry, Bruce Blank, and Michel Boileau
| "for using postage stamps to test whether the male sexual organ is functioning properly—as described in their study ''Nocturnal Penile Tumescence Monitoring With Stamps''"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Barry | first1=John M. | last2=Blank | first2=Bruce | last3=Boileau | first3=Michael | title=Nocturnal penile tumescence monitoring with stamps | journal=Urology | volume=15 | issue=2 | date=1980 | pmid=7355543 | doi=10.1016/0090-4295(80)90414-8 | pages=171–172 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/0090429580904148 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Economics
| Lindie Hanyu Liang, Douglas Brown, Huiwen Lian, Samuel Hanig, D. Lance Ferris, and Lisa Keeping
| "for investigating whether it is effective for employees to use [[Voodoo doll]]s to retaliate against abusive bosses"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Liang | first1=Lindie H. | last2=Brown | first2=Douglas J. | last3=Lian | first3=Huiwen | last4=Hanig | first4=Samuel | last5=Ferris | first5=D. Lance | last6=Keeping | first6=Lisa M. | title=Righting a wrong: Retaliation on a voodoo doll symbolizing an abusive supervisor restores justice | journal=The Leadership Quarterly | volume=29 | issue=4 | date=2018 | doi=10.1016/j.leaqua.2018.01.004 | pages=443–456 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S104898431730276X | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|}

== 2019 ==
[[File:The 29th First Annual Ig Nobel Prize Ceremony (2019).webm|thumb|thumbtime=6:26|Video of the 2019 Ceremony]]
The ceremony took place on 12 September 2019.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.improbable.com/2019/09/12/announcing-the-2019-ig-nobel-prize-winners/|title=Announcing the 2019 Ig Nobel Prize Winners|date=13 September 2019|website=Improbable Research|access-date=13 September 2019}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Medicine
| Silvano Gallus
| "for collecting evidence that pizza might protect against illness and death, if the pizza is made and eaten in Italy"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Gallus | first1=Silvano | last2=Bosetti | first2=Cristina | last3=Negri | first3=Eva | last4=Talamini | first4=Renato | last5=Montella | first5=Maurizio | last6=Conti | first6=Ettore | last7=Franceschi | first7=Silvia | last8=La Vecchia | first8=Carlo | title=Does pizza protect against cancer? | journal=International Journal of Cancer | volume=107 | issue=2 | date=2003 | issn=0020-7136 | doi=10.1002/ijc.11382 | pages=283–284 | pmid=12949808 | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ijc.11382 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Gallus | first1=S | last2=Tavani | first2=A | last3=Vecchia | first3=C La | title=Pizza and risk of acute myocardial infarction | journal=European Journal of Clinical Nutrition | volume=58 | issue=11 | date=2004-11-01 | issn=0954-3007 | doi=10.1038/sj.ejcn.1601997 | pages=1543–1546 | pmid=15138460 | url=https://www.nature.com/articles/1601997 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Gallus | first1=Silvano | last2=Talamini | first2=Renato | last3=Bosetti | first3=Cristina | last4=Negri | first4=Eva | last5=Montella | first5=Maurizio | last6=Franceschi | first6=Silvia | last7=Giacosa | first7=Attilio | last8=La Vecchia | first8=Carlo | title=Pizza consumption and the risk of breast, ovarian and prostate cancer | journal=European Journal of Cancer Prevention | volume=15 | issue=1 | date=2006 | issn=0959-8278 | doi=10.1097/01.cej.0000186632.04625.f6 | pages=74–76 | pmid=16374234 | url=https://journals.lww.com/00008469-200602000-00012 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Medical Education
| Karen Pryor and Theresa McKeon
| "for using a simple animal-training technique—called ''[[clicker training]]''—to help new doctors acquire basic surgical skills"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Levy | first1=Martin I. | last2=Pryor | first2=Karen W. | last3=McKeon | first3=Theresa R. | title=Is Teaching Simple Surgical Skills Using an Operant Learning Program More Effective Than Teaching by Demonstration? | journal=Clinical Orthopaedics & Related Research | volume=474 | issue=4 | date=2016 | issn=0009-921X | pmid=26369658 | pmc=4773331 | doi=10.1007/s11999-015-4555-8 | pages=945–955 }}</ref>
|-
| Biology
| Ling-Jun Kong, Herbert Crepaz, Agnieszka Górecka, Aleksandra Urbanek, Rainer Dumke, and Tomasz Paterek
| "for discovering that dead magnetized [[cockroach]]es behave differently than living magnetized cockroaches"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Kong | first1=Ling-Jun | last2=Crepaz | first2=Herbert | last3=Górecka | first3=Agnieszka | last4=Urbanek | first4=Aleksandra | last5=Dumke | first5=Rainer | last6=Paterek | first6=Tomasz | title=In-vivo biomagnetic characterisation of the American cockroach | journal=Scientific Reports | volume=8 | issue=1 | date=2018-03-23 | issn=2045-2322 | pmid=29572509 | pmc=5865160 | doi=10.1038/s41598-018-23005-1 | article-number=5140 | arxiv=1702.00538 | bibcode=2018NatSR...8.5140K }}</ref>
|-
| Anatomy
| Roger Mieusset and Bourras Bengoudifa
| "for measuring [[Scrotum#Asymmetry|scrotal temperature asymmetry]] in naked and clothed postmen in France"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Bengoudifa | first1=B. | last2=Mieusset | first2=R. | title=Thermal asymmetry of the human scrotum | journal=Human Reproduction | volume=22 | issue=8 | date=2007-08-01 | pmid=17623724 | issn=1460-2350 | doi=10.1093/humrep/dem133 | pages=2178–2182 | url=http://academic.oup.com/humrep/article/22/8/2178/643997/Thermal-asymmetry-of-the-human-scrotum | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Chemistry
| Shigeru Watanabe, Mineko Ohnishi, Kaori Imai, Eiji Kawano, and Seiji Igarashi
| "for estimating the total [[Saliva#Production|saliva volume produced]] per day by a typical five-year-old child"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Watanabe | first1=S. | last2=Ohnishi | first2=M. | last3=Imai | first3=K. | last4=Kawano | first4=E. | last5=Igarashi | first5=S. | title=Estimation of the total saliva volume produced per day in five-year-old children | journal=Archives of Oral Biology | volume=40 | issue=8 | date=1995 | pmid=7487581 | issn=0003-9969 | doi=10.1016/0003-9969(95)00026-L | pages=781–782 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/000399699500026L | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>{{efn|The award ceremony was attended by Shiguru Watanabe and his adult sons, who were some of the subjects of the study when they were children 35 years prior.}}
|-
| Engineering
| Iman Farahbakhsh
| "for inventing a diaper-changing machine for use on human infants"
| <ref>{{cite web | title=Infant washer and diaper-changer apparatus and method | website=Google Patents | date=2017-01-13 | url=https://patents.google.com/patent/US20170143168 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Economics
| Habip Gedik, Timothy A. Voss, and Andreas Voss
| "for testing which country's paper money is best at transmitting dangerous bacteria"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Gedik | first1=Habip | last2=Voss | first2=Timothy A | last3=Voss | first3=Andreas | title=Money and transmission of bacteria | journal=Antimicrobial Resistance and Infection Control | volume=2 | issue=1 | date=2013 | issn=2047-2994 | pmc=3765964 | pmid=23985137 | doi=10.1186/2047-2994-2-22 | doi-access=free | article-number=22 }}</ref>
|-
| Peace
| Ghada A. bin Saif, Alexandru Papoiu, Liliana Banari, [[Francis McGlone]], Shawn G. Kwatra, Yiong-Huak Chan, and Gil Yosipovitch
| "for trying to measure the pleasurability of [[Scratch reflex|scratching an itch]]"
| <ref name="l748">{{cite journal | last1=bin Saif | first1=G.A. | last2=Papoiu | first2=A.D.P. | last3=Banari | first3=L. | last4=McGlone | first4=F. | last5=Kwatra | first5=S.G. | last6=Chan | first6=Y-H. | last7=Yosipovitch | first7=G. | title=The pleasurability of scratching an itch: a psychophysical and topographical assessment | journal=British Journal of Dermatology | volume=166 | issue=5 | date=2012 | issn=0007-0963 | pmid=22242789 | pmc=3335970 | doi=10.1111/j.1365-2133.2012.10826.x | pages=981–985 }}</ref>
|-
| Psychology
| [[Fritz Strack]]
| "for discovering that holding a pen in one's mouth makes one smile, which makes one happier — and for then discovering that it does not"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Strack | first1=Fritz | last2=Martin | first2=Leonard L. | last3=Stepper | first3=Sabine | title=Inhibiting and facilitating conditions of the human smile: A nonobtrusive test of the facial feedback hypothesis. | journal=Journal of Personality and Social Psychology | volume=54 | issue=5 | date=1988 | issn=1939-1315 | pmid=3379579 | s2cid=15291233 |doi=10.1037/0022-3514.54.5.768 | pages=768–777 | url=https://doi.apa.org/doi/10.1037/0022-3514.54.5.768 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last=Strack | first=Fritz | title=From Data to Truth in Psychological Science. A Personal Perspective. | journal=Frontiers in Psychology | volume=8 | date=2017-05-16 | issn=1664-1078 | pmid=28559859 | pmc=5432643 | doi=10.3389/fpsyg.2017.00702 | doi-access=free | article-number=702 }}</ref>
|-
| Physics
| Patricia Yang, Alexander Lee, Miles Chan, Alynn Martin, Ashley Edwards, Scott Carver, and [[David Hu (scientist)|David Hu]]
| "for studying how, and why, wombats [[Wombat feces|make cube-shaped poo]]"
| <ref>{{Cite journal|title=71st Annual Meeting of the APS Division of Fluid Dynamics – Event – How do wombats make cubed poo?|url=http://meetings.aps.org/Meeting/DFD18/Event/333058|journal=Bulletin of the American Physical Society|publisher=American Physical Society|volume=63|issue=13}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Yang | first1=Patricia J. | last2=Lee | first2=Alexander B. | last3=Chan | first3=Miles | last4=Kowalski | first4=Michael | last5=Qiu | first5=Kelly | last6=Waid | first6=Christopher | last7=Cervantes | first7=Gabriel | last8=Magondu | first8=Benjamin | last9=Biagioni | first9=Morgan | last10=Vogelnest | first10=Larry | last11=Martin | first11=Alynn | last12=Edwards | first12=Ashley | last13=Carver | first13=Scott | last14=Hu | first14=David L. | title=Intestines of non-uniform stiffness mold the corners of wombat feces | journal=Soft Matter | volume=17 | issue=3 | date=2021 | issn=1744-683X | doi=10.1039/D0SM01230K | pages=475–488 | pmid=33289747 | bibcode=2021SMat...17..475Y | url=https://xlink.rsc.org/?DOI=D0SM01230K | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|}

== 2020 ==
The ceremony took place on 17 September 2020 and was [[webcast]].<ref name="arstechnica2020">{{Cite web |last=Ouellette |first=Jennifer |date=2020-09-17 |title=Here are the winners of the 2020 Ig Nobel Prizes to make you laugh, then think |url=https://arstechnica.com/science/2020/09/bellowing-alligators-and-frozen-poop-knives-the-2020-ig-nobel-prizes/ |access-date=2024-09-25 |website=Ars Technica |language=en-us}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Acoustics
| Stephan Reber, Takeshi Nishimura, Judith Janisch, Mark Robertson, and Tecumseh Fitch,
| "for inducing a female [[Chinese alligator]] to bellow in an airtight chamber filled with helium-enriched air"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Reber | first1=Stephan A. | last2=Nishimura | first2=Takeshi | last3=Janisch | first3=Judith | last4=Robertson | first4=Mark | last5=Fitch | first5=W. Tecumseh | title=A Chinese alligator in heliox: formant frequencies in a crocodilian | journal=Journal of Experimental Biology | volume=218 | issue=15 | date=2015-08-01 | issn=1477-9145 | pmid=26246611 | pmc=4528706 | doi=10.1242/jeb.119552 | pages=2442–2447 | bibcode=2015JExpB.218.2442R }}</ref>

| Economics
| Christopher Watkins, Juan David Leongómez, Jeanne Bovet, Agnieszka Żelaźniewicz, Max Korbmacher, Marco Antônio Corrêa Varella, Ana Maria Fernandez, Danielle Wagstaff, and Samuela Bolgan
| "for trying to quantify the relationship between different countries' national income inequality and the average amount of mouth-to-mouth kissing"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Watkins | first1=Christopher D. | last2=Leongómez | first2=Juan David | last3=Bovet | first3=Jeanne | last4=Żelaźniewicz | first4=Agnieszka | last5=Korbmacher | first5=Max | last6=Varella | first6=Marco Antônio Corrêa | last7=Fernandez | first7=Ana Maria | last8=Wagstaff | first8=Danielle | last9=Bolgan | first9=Samuela | title=National income inequality predicts cultural variation in mouth to mouth kissing | journal=Scientific Reports | volume=9 | issue=1 | date=2019-04-30 | issn=2045-2322 | pmid=31040378 | pmc=6491799 | doi=10.1038/s41598-019-43267-7 | article-number=6698 | bibcode=2019NatSR...9.6698W }}</ref>
|-
| Entomology
| Richard Vetter
| "for collecting evidence that many entomologists (scientists who study insects) are afraid of spiders, which are not insects"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last=Vetter | first=Richard S. | title=Arachnophobic Entomologists: When Two More Legs Makes a Big Difference | journal=American Entomologist | volume=59 | issue=3 | date=2013 | issn=2155-9902 | doi=10.1093/ae/59.3.168 | pages=168–175 | url=https://academic.oup.com/ae/article-lookup/doi/10.1093/ae/59.3.168 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Management
| Xi Guang-An, Mo Tian-Xiang, Yang Kang-Sheng, Yang Guang-Sheng, and Ling Xian Si
| "five professional hitmen in Guangxi, China, who managed a contract for a [[hit job]] (a murder performed for money) in the following way: After accepting payment to perform the murder, Xi Guang-An then instead subcontracted the task to Mo Tian-Xiang, who then instead subcontracted the task to Yang Kang-Sheng, who then instead subcontracted the task to Yang Guang-Sheng, who then instead subcontracted the task to Ling Xian-Si, with each subsequently enlisted hitman receiving a smaller percentage of the fee, and nobody actually performing a murder"
| <ref name="arstechnica2020"/>
|-
| Materials Science
| Metin Eren, Michelle Bebber, James Norris, Alyssa Perrone, Ashley Rutkoski, Michael Wilson, and Mary Ann Raghanti
| "for showing that knives manufactured from frozen human feces do not work well"
|<ref>{{cite journal | last1=Eren | first1=Metin I. | last2=Bebber | first2=Michelle R. | last3=Norris | first3=James D. | last4=Perrone | first4=Alyssa | last5=Rutkoski | first5=Ashley | last6=Wilson | first6=Michael | last7=Raghanti | first7=Mary Ann | title=Experimental replication shows knives manufactured from frozen human feces do not work | journal=Journal of Archaeological Science: Reports | volume=27 | date=2019 | doi=10.1016/j.jasrep.2019.102002 | article-number=102002 | doi-access=free | bibcode=2019JArSR..2702002E }}</ref>
|-
| Medicine
| Nienke Vulink, Damiaan Denys, and Arnoud van Loon
| "for diagnosing a long-unrecognized medical condition: [[Misophonia]], the distress at hearing other people make chewing sounds"
| <ref>{{cite journal |last1=Schröder |first1=Arjan |last2=Vulink |first2=Nienke |last3=Denys |first3=Damiaan |title=Misophonia: Diagnostic Criteria for a New Psychiatric Disorder |journal=PLOS ONE |date=23 January 2013 |volume=8 |issue=1 |article-number=e54706 |doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0054706 |pmid=23372758 |pmc=3553052 |bibcode=2013PLoSO...854706S |issn=1932-6203|doi-access=free }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Schröder |first1=Arjan E. |last2=Vulink |first2=Nienke C. |last3=van Loon |first3=Arnoud J. |last4=Denys |first4=Damiaan A. |title=Cognitive behavioral therapy is effective in misophonia: An open trial |journal=Journal of Affective Disorders |date=1 August 2017 |volume=217 |pages=289–294 |doi=10.1016/j.jad.2017.04.017 |pmid=28441620 |s2cid=4871003 |url=https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0165032716321681 |issn=0165-0327|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Medical Education
| [[Jair Bolsonaro]] of Brazil, [[Boris Johnson]] of the United Kingdom, [[Narendra Modi]] of India, [[Andrés Manuel López Obrador]] of Mexico, [[Alexander Lukashenko]] of Belarus, [[Donald Trump]] of the United States, [[Recep Tayyip Erdoğan]] of Turkey, [[Vladimir Putin]] of Russia, and [[Gurbanguly Berdimuhamedow]] of Turkmenistan

| Peace
| The governments of [[India]] and [[Pakistan]]
| "for having their diplomats surreptitiously [[Knock, Knock, Ginger|ring each other's doorbells in the middle of the night, and then run away before anyone had a chance to answer the door]]"
| <ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2018/mar/16/pakistan-recalls-envoy-from-india-harassment-claims-doorbell-ringing|title=Pakistan recalls envoy from India in ding-dong over harassment claims|date=16 March 2018|access-date=17 March 2021|work=The Guardian|first=Michael|last=Safi}}</ref>
|-
| Physics
| Ivan Maksymov and Andrey Pototsky
| "for determining, experimentally, what happens to the shape of a living [[earthworm]] when one vibrates the earthworm at high frequency"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Maksymov | first1=Ivan S. | last2=Pototsky | first2=Andrey | title=Excitation of Faraday-like body waves in vibrated living earthworms | journal=Scientific Reports | volume=10 | issue=1 | date=2020-05-22 | issn=2045-2322 | pmid=32444625 | pmc=7244598 | doi=10.1038/s41598-020-65295-4 | article-number=8564 | arxiv=1912.07593 | bibcode=2020NatSR..10.8564M }}</ref>

| Psychology
| Miranda Giacomin and Nicholas Rule
| "for devising a method to identify [[Narcissism|narcissists]] by examining their eyebrows"

== 2021 ==
The 31st First Annual Ig Nobel Prize ceremony took place on Thursday, 9 September 2021 and was webcast.<ref>{{cite web|title=The 31st First Annual Ig Nobel Prize Ceremony 2021| website=[[YouTube]] | date=9 September 2021 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_Rr8NxPDzBM |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211212/_Rr8NxPDzBM| archive-date=2021-12-12 |url-status=live|language=en|access-date=2021-09-10}}{{cbignore}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2006-08-01|title=Past Ig Winners|url=https://www.improbable.com/2021-ceremony/winners/|access-date=2021-09-10|website=Improbable Research}}</ref>

| Biology
| Susanne Schötz, Robert Eklund, and Joost van de Weijer
| "for analyzing variations in purring, chirping, chattering, trilling, tweedling, murmuring, meowing, moaning, squeaking, hissing, yowling, howling, growling, and other modes of cat–human communication"
| <ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Schötz|first1=Susanne|last2=Eklund|first2=Robert|date=2011|title=A comparative acoustic analysis of purring in four cats|url=https://www.ida.liu.se/~robek28/pdf/Schotz_Eklund_2011_Purring_DomesticCats.pdf|journal=Quarterly Progress and Status Report TMH-QPSR, Volume 51, 2011. 2012|language=English|publisher=University of Gothenburg|pages=45–48}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Schötz|first=Susanne|year=2013|title=A phonetic pilot study of chirp, chatter, tweet and tweedle in | Ecology
| Leila Satari, Alba Guillén, Àngela Vidal-Verdú, and Manuel Porcar
| "for using [[genetic analysis]] to identify the different species of bacteria that reside in wads of discarded chewing gum stuck on pavements in various countries"

| Chemistry
| Jörg Wicker, Nicolas Krauter, Bettina Derstroff, Christof Stönner, Efstratios Bourtsoukidis, Achim Edtbauer, Jochen Wulf, Thomas Klüpfel, Stefan Kramer, and Jonathan Williams
| "for chemically analyzing the air inside movie theaters, to test whether the odors produced by an audience reliably indicate the levels of violence, sex, antisocial behavior, drug use, and bad language in the movie the audience is watching"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Stönner | first1=C. | last2=Edtbauer | first2=A. | last3=Derstroff | first3=B. | last4=Bourtsoukidis | first4=E. | last5=Klüpfel | first5=T. | last6=Wicker | first6=J. | last7=Williams | first7=J. | title=Proof of concept study: Testing human volatile organic compounds as tools for age classification of films | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=13 | issue=10 | date=2018-10-11 | issn=1932-6203 | pmid=30307954 | pmc=6181293 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0203044 | doi-access=free | article-number=e0203044 | bibcode=2018PLoSO..1303044S }}</ref><ref>{{cite conference | last1=Wicker | first1=Jörg | last2=Krauter | first2=Nicolas | last3=Derstorff | first3=Bettina | last4=Stönner | first4=Christof | last5=Bourtsoukidis | first5=Efstratios | last6=Klüpfel | first6=Thomas | last7=Williams | first7=Jonathan | last8=Kramer | first8=Stefan | title=Cinema Data Mining: The Smell of Fear | publisher=ACM | date=2015-08-10 | isbn=978-1-4503-3664-2 | doi=10.1145/2783258.2783404 | url=https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/2783258.2783404 | access-date=2025-12-14 | pages=1295–1304| url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Pavlo Blavatskyy

| Medicine

M. | title=A New Method of Cockroach Control on Submarines12 | journal=Journal of Economic Entomology | volume=64 | issue=5 | date=1971-10-15 | issn=1938-291X | doi=10.1093/jee/64.5.1196 | pages=1196–1198 | pmid=5122339 | url=http://academic.oup.com/jee/article/64/5/1196/2210530/A-New-Method-of-Cockroach-Control-on-Submarines12 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
