| Transportation
| Robin Radcliffe, Mark Jago, Peter Morkel, Estelle Morkel, Pierre du Preez, Piet Beytell, Birgit Kotting, Bakker Manuel, Jan Hendrik du Preez, Michele Miller, Julia Felippe, Stephen Parry, and Robin Gleed
| "for determining by experiment whether it is safer to transport an airborne [[rhinoceros]] upside-down"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Radcliffe | first1=Robin W. | last2=Jago | first2=Mark | last3=Morkel | first3=Peter vdB | last4=Morkel | first4=Estelle | last5=du Preez | first5=Pierre | last6=Beytell | first6=Piet | last7=Kotting | first7=Birgit | last8=Manuel | first8=Bakker | last9=du Preez | first9=Jan Hendrik | last10=Miller | first10=Michele A. | last11=Felippe | first11=Julia | last12=Parry | first12=Stephen A. | last13=Gleed | first13=Robin D. | title=The Pulmonary and Metabolic Effects of Suspension by the Feet Compared with Lateral Recumbency in Immobilized Black Rhinoceroses (Diceros Bicornis) Captured by Aerial Darting| journal=Journal of Wildlife Diseases | volume=57 | issue=2 | date=2021-03-25 | pages=357–367 | issn=0090-3558 | doi=10.7589/2019-08-202 | url=https://meridian.allenpress.com/jwd/article/57/2/357/451340/THE-PULMONARY-AND-METABOLIC-EFFECTS-OF-SUSPENSION | access-date=2025-12-14 | pmid=33822147 | url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|}

== 2022 ==
The 32nd First Annual Ig Nobel Prize ceremony took place on Thursday, 15 September 2022, and was presented in a [[webcast]] format.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2022-09-16 |title=Swedish moose crash-test dummy wins spoof Ig Nobel prize |language=en-GB |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-62915382 |access-date=2022-09-16}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Applied Cardiology
| Eliska Prochazkova, Elio Sjak-Shie, Friederike Behrens, Daniel Lindh, and Mariska Kret
| "for seeking and finding evidence that when new romantic partners meet for the first time, and feel attracted to each other, their heart rates synchronize"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Prochazkova | first1=E. | last2=Sjak-Shie | first2=E. | last3=Behrens | first3=F. | last4=Lindh | first4=D. | last5=Kret | first5=M. E. | title=Physiological synchrony is associated with attraction in a blind date setting | journal=Nature Human Behaviour | volume=6 | issue=2 | date=2021-11-01 | issn=2397-3374 | doi=10.1038/s41562-021-01197-3 | pages=269–278 | pmid=34725513 | url=https://www.nature.com/articles/s41562-021-01197-3 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Literature
| Eric Martínez, Francis Mollica, and Edward Gibson
| "for analyzing what makes legal documents unnecessarily difficult to understand"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=Martínez | first1=Eric | last2=Mollica | first2=Francis | last3=Gibson | first3=Edward | title=Poor writing, not specialized concepts, drives processing difficulty in legal language | journal=Cognition | volume=224 | date=2022 | doi=10.1016/j.cognition.2022.105070 | article-number=105070 | doi-access=free | pmid=35257980 }}</ref>
|-
| Biology
| Solimary García-Hernández and Glauco Machado
| "for studying whether and how [[constipation]] affects the mating prospects of [[scorpion]]s"
| <ref>{{cite journal | last1=García-Hernández | first1=Solimary | last2=Machado | first2=Glauco | title=Short- and long-term effects of an extreme case of autotomy: does "tail" loss and subsequent constipation decrease the locomotor performance of male and female scorpions? | journal=Integrative Zoology | volume=17 | issue=5 | date=2022 | issn=1749-4877 | doi=10.1111/1749-4877.12604 | pages=672–688 | pmid=34741423 | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/1749-4877.12604 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=García-Hernández | first1=Solimary | last2=Machado | first2=Glauco | title=Fitness Implications of Nonlethal Injuries in Scorpions: Females, but Not Males, Pay Reproductive Costs | journal=The American Naturalist | volume=197 | issue=3 | date=2021-03-01 | issn=0003-0147 | doi=10.1086/712759 | pages=379–389 | pmid=33625967 | bibcode=2021ANat..197..379G | url=https://www.journals.uchicago.edu/doi/10.1086/712759 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=García-Hernández | first1=Solimary | last2=Machado | first2=Glauco | title='Tail' autotomy and consequent stinger loss decrease predation success in scorpions | journal=Animal Behaviour | volume=169 | date=2020 | doi=10.1016/j.anbehav.2020.08.019 | pages=157–167 | bibcode=2020AnBeh.169..157G | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0003347220302621 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Medicine

== 2023 ==
The 33rd First Annual Ig Nobel Prize ceremony took place on Thursday, 14 September 2023, and was presented in webcast.<ref>{{Cite report |url=https://www.science.org/content/article/ig-nobel-prizes-honor-zombie-spiders-rock-licking-scientists-and-clever-commode |title=Ig Nobel Prizes honor zombie spiders, rock-licking scientists, and a clever commode |last=Jacobs |first=Phie |date=2023-09-14 |doi=10.1126/science.adk8631 |language=en|url-access=subscription }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last1=Davis |first1=Nicola |date=2023-09-14 |title=Reanimated spiders and smart toilets triumph at Ig Nobel prizes |language=en-GB |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/science/2023/sep/14/reanimated-spiders-and-smart-toilets-triumph-at-ig-nobel-prizes |access-date=2023-09-15 |issn=0261-3077}}</ref>

==2024==
The 34th First Annual Ig Nobel Prize ceremony took place on Thursday, 12 September 2024, and was held at the [[Massachusetts Institute of Technology]].<ref name="guardian2024">{{Cite news |last1=Sample |first1=Ian |date=2024-09-13 |title=Ig Nobel prize goes to team who found mammals can breathe through anuses |language=en-GB |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/science/2024/sep/12/ig-nobel-prize-goes-to-team-who-found-mammals-can-breathe-through-anuses |access-date=2024-09-13}}</ref>

! scope="col"| Refs

| Anatomy
|  Marjolaine Willems, Quentin Hennocq, Sara Tunon de Lara, Nicolas Kogane, Vincent Fleury, Romy Rayssiguier, Juan José Cortés Santander, Roberto Requena, Julien Stirnemann, and Roman Hossein Khonsari
| "for studying whether the hair on the heads of most people in the [[northern hemisphere]] [[hair whorl|swirls in the same direction]] (clockwise or counter-clockwise?) as hair on the heads of most people in the [[southern hemisphere]]."
|<ref name="k221">{{cite journal | last1=Willems | first1=Marjolaine | last2=Hennocq | first2=Quentin | last3=de Lara | first3=Sara Tunon | last4=Kogane | first4=Nicolas | last5=Fleury | first5=Vincent | last6=Rayssiguier | first6=Romy | last7=Santander | first7=Juan José Cortés | last8=Requena | first8=Roberto | last9=Stirnemann | first9=Julien | last10=Khonsari | first10=Roman Hossein | title=Genetic determinism and hemispheric influence in hair whorl formation | journal=Journal of Stomatology, Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery | volume=125 | issue=2 | date=2024 | doi=10.1016/j.jormas.2023.101664 | article-number=101664 | pmid=37913994 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S2468785523002859 | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Peace 
| [[B. F. Skinner]]{{dagger|sup=yes}}

| Physics
| James C. Liao
| "for demonstrating and explaining the swimming abilities of a dead [[trout]]"
| <ref name="q111">{{cite journal | last=Liao | first=James C. | title=Neuromuscular control of trout swimming in a vortex street: implications for energy economy during the Kármán gait | journal=Journal of Experimental Biology | volume=207 | issue=20 | date=2004-09-15 | issn=1477-9145 | doi=10.1242/jeb.01125 | pages=3495–3506 | pmid=15339945 | bibcode=2004JExpB.207.3495L | url=https://journals.biologists.com/jeb/article/207/20/3495/14915/Neuromuscular-control-of-trout-swimming-in-a | access-date=2025-12-14| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Physiology
| Ryo Okabe, Toyofumi F. Chen-Yoshikawa, Yosuke Yoneyama, Yuhei Yokoyama, Satona Tanaka, Akihiko Yoshizawa, Wendy L. Thompson, Gokul Kannan, Eiji Kobayashi, Hiroshi Date, and Takanori Takebe
| "for discovering that many mammals are capable of [[Enteral respiration|breathing through their anus]]"
| <ref name="d372">{{cite journal | last1=Okabe | first1=Ryo | last2=Chen-Yoshikawa | first2=Toyofumi F. | last3=Yoneyama | first3=Yosuke | last4=Yokoyama | first4=Yuhei | last5=Tanaka | first5=Satona | last6=Yoshizawa | first6=Akihiko | last7=Thompson | first7=Wendy L. | last8=Kannan | first8=Gokul | last9=Kobayashi | first9=Eiji | last10=Date | first10=Hiroshi | last11=Takebe | first11=Takanori | title=Mammalian enteral ventilation ameliorates respiratory failure | journal=Med | volume=2 | issue=6 | date=2021 | doi=10.1016/j.medj.2021.04.004 | pages=773–783.e5 | pmid=35590139 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S2666634021001537 | access-date=2025-12-14}}</ref>
|-
| Probability
| František Bartoš, Eric-Jan Wagenmakers, Alexandra Sarafoglou, Henrik Godmann, and many colleagues
| "for showing, both in theory and by 350,757 experiments, that when you flip a coin, it tends to land on the same side as it started"
| <ref name="b711">{{cite journal | last1=Bartoš | first1=František | last2=Sarafoglou | first2=Alexandra | last3=Godmann | first3=Henrik R. | last4=Sahrani | first4=Amir | last5=Klein Leunk | first5=David | last6=Gui | first6=Pierre Y. | last7=Voss | first7=David | last8=Ullah | first8=Kaleem | last9=Zoubek | first9=Malte | last10=Nippold | first10=Franziska | last11=Aust | first11=Frederik | last12=Vieira | first12=Felipe Fontana | last13=Islam | first13=Chris-Gabriel | last14=Zoubek | first14=Anton J. | last15=Shabani | first15=Sara | last16=Petter | first16=Jonas | last17=Roos | first17=Ingeborg B. | last18=Finnemann | first18=Adam | last19=Lob | first19=Aaron B. | last20=Hoffstadt | first20=Madlen F. | last21=Nak | first21=Jason | last22=de Ron | first22=Jill | last23=Derks | first23=Koen | last24=Huth | first24=Karoline | last25=Terpstra | first25=Sjoerd | last26=Bastelica | first26=Thomas | last27=Matetovici | first27=Magda | last28=Ott | first28=Vincent L. | last29=Zetea | first29=Andreea S. | last30=Karnbach | first30=Katharina | last31=Donzallaz | first31=Michelle C. | last32=John | first32=Arne | last33=Moore | first33=Roy M. | last34=Assion | first34=Franziska | last35=van Bork | first35=Riet | last36=Leidinger | first36=Theresa E. | last37=Zhao | first37=Xiaochang | last38=Karami Motaghi | first38=Adrian | last39=Pan | first39=Ting | last40=Armstrong | first40=Hannah | last41=Peng | first41=Tianqi | last42=Bialas | first42=Mara | last43=Pang | first43=Joyce Y.-C. | last44=Fu | first44=Bohan | last45=Yang | first45=Shujun | last46=Lin | first46=Xiaoyi | last47=Sleiffer | first47=Dana | last48=Bognar | first48=Miklos | last49=Aczel | first49=Balazs | last50=Wagenmakers | first50=Eric-Jan | title=Fair Coins Tend to Land on the Same Side They Started: Evidence from 350,757 Flips | journal=Journal of the American Statistical Association | date=2025-08-11 | volume=120 | issue=552 | issn=0162-1459 | |}

==2025==
The 35th First Annual Ig Nobel Prize ceremony took place on Thursday, 18 September 2025, and was held at [[Boston University]].<ref name="bbc2025">{{Cite web |date=2024-09-13 |title=Zebra cows and Teflon food make Ig Nobel prize winners |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/crkjzxrrkd5o |access-date=2025-09-20 |website=bbc.com |language=en-GB }}</ref>

{| class="wikitable plainrowheaders unsortable" style="width:100%;"
! scope="col"| Category
! scope="col"| Winner(s)
! scope="col"| Rationale
! scope="col"| Refs
|- 
| Aviation
| Francisco Sánchez, Mariana Melcón, Carmi Korine, and Berry Pinshow
| "for studying whether ingesting [[Alcoholic beverage|alcohol]] can impair [[bats]]' ability to fly and also their ability to echolocate"
| <ref name="z036">{{cite journal | last1=Sánchez | first1=Francisco | last2=Melcón | first2=Mariana | last3=Korine | first3=Carmi | last4=Pinshow | first4=Berry | title=Ethanol ingestion affects flight performance and echolocation in Egyptian fruit bats | journal=Behavioural Processes | volume=84 | issue=2 | date=2010 | doi=10.1016/j.beproc.2010.02.006 | pages=555–558 | pmid=20153407 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0376635710000446 | access-date=2025-12-13| url-access=subscription }}</ref>
|-
| Biology
| Tomoki Kojima, Kazato Oishi, Yasushi Matsubara, Yuki Uchiyama, Yoshihiko Fukushima, Naoto Aoki, Say Sato, Tatsuaki Masuda, Junichi Ueda, Hiroyuki Hirooka, and Katsutoshi Kino
| "for their experiments to learn whether [[cows]] painted with [[zebra stripes|zebra-like striping]] can avoid being bitten by [[flies]]"
| <ref name="i513">{{cite journal | last1=Kojima | first1=Tomoki | last2=Oishi | first2=Kazato | last3=Matsubara | first3=Yasushi | last4=Uchiyama | first4=Yuki | last5=Fukushima | first5=Yoshihiko | last6=Aoki | first6=Naoto | last7=Sato | first7=Say | last8=Masuda | first8=Tatsuaki | last9=Ueda | first9=Junichi | last10=Hirooka | first10=Hiroyuki | last11=Kino | first11=Katsutoshi | title=Cows painted with zebra-like striping can avoid biting fly attack | journal=PLOS ONE | volume=14 | issue=10 | date=2019-10-03 | issn=1932-6203 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0223447 | doi-access=free | article-number=e0223447| pmid=31581218 | pmc=6776349 | bibcode=2019PLoSO..1423447K }}</ref>{{efn|This prize builds on research by ''Horváth et al. (2010)'', the authors of whom were awarded the 2016 Ig Nobel Physics Prize.}}
|-
| Chemistry
| Rotem Naftalovich, Daniel Naftalovich, and Frank Greenway
| "for experiments to test whether eating [[Teflon]] is a good way to increase food volume and hence satiety without increasing calorie content."
| <ref name="f654">{{cite journal | last1=Naftalovich | first1=Rotem | last2=Naftalovich | first2=Daniel | last3=Greenway | first3=Frank L. | title=Polytetrafluoroethylene Ingestion as a Way to Increase Food Volume and Hence Satiety Without Increasing Calorie Content | journal=Journal of Diabetes Science and Technology | volume=10 | issue=4 | date=2016 | issn=1932-2968 | pmid=26810925 | pmc=4928218 | doi=10.1177/1932296815626726 | pages=971–976 }}</ref>
|-
| Engineering design
| Vikash Kumar and Sarthak Mittal
| "for analyzing, from an [[engineering design]] perspective, how [[Foot odor|foul-smelling shoes]] affect the good experience of using a [[shoe rack|shoe-rack]]"
| <ref name="d123">{{cite book | last1=Kumar | first1=Vikash | last2=Mittal | first2=Sarthak | title=Ergonomics for Improved Productivity | chapter=Smelly Shoes—An Opportunity for Shoe Rack Re-Design | publisher=Springer Nature Singapore | publication-place=Singapore | date=2022 | isbn=978-981-16-2228-1 | doi=10.1007/978-981-16-2229-8_33 | chapter-url=https://link.springer.com/10.1007/978-981-16-2229-8_33 | access-date=2025-12-13 | pages=287–293}}</ref>

| Literature
| [[William Bennett Bean|William B. Bean]]
| "for persistently recording and analyzing the [[Development of the human body|rate of growth]] of one of his [[fingernails]] over a period of 35 years"
| <ref name="f828">{{cite journal | last=Bean | first=William B. | title=Nail Growth: Thirty-five Years of Observation | journal=Archives of Internal Medicine | volume=140 | issue=1 | date=1980-01-01 | pages=73–76 | issn=0003-9926 | doi=10.1001/archinte.1980.00330130075019 | pmid=7352807 | url=http://archinte.jamanetwork.com/article.aspx?doi=10.1001/archinte.1980.00330130075019 | access-date=2025-12-13| url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Nutrition
| Daniele Dendi, Gabriel H. Segniagbeto, Roger Meek, and [[Luca Luiselli]]
| "for studying the extent to which [[Agama agama|a certain kind of lizard]] chooses to eat certain kinds of [[pizza]]"
| <ref name="n960">{{cite journal | last1=Dendi | first1=Daniele | last2=Segniagbeto | first2=Gabriel H. | last3=Meek | first3=Roger | last4=Luiselli | first4=Luca | title=Opportunistic foraging strategy of rainbow lizards at a seaside resort in Togo | journal=African Journal of Ecology | volume=61 | issue=1 | date=2023 | issn=0141-6707 | doi=10.1111/aje.13100 | pages=226–227 | bibcode=2023AfJEc..61..226D | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/aje.13100 | access-date=2025-12-13| url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Peace
| Fritz Renner, Inge Kersbergen, Matt Field, and Jessica Werthmann
| "for showing that drinking [[Alcoholic beverage|alcohol]] sometimes improves a person's ability to speak in a [[foreign language]]"
| <ref name="i266">{{cite journal | last1=Renner | first1=Fritz | last2=Kersbergen | first2=Inge | last3=Field | first3=Matt | last4=Werthmann | first4=Jessica | title=Dutch courage? Effects of acute alcohol consumption on self-ratings and observer ratings of foreign language skills | journal=Journal of Psychopharmacology | volume=32 | issue=1 | date=2018 | issn=0269-8811 | doi=10.1177/0269881117735687 | pages=116–122 | pmid=29043911 | url=https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/0269881117735687 | access-date=2025-12-13| url-access=subscription }}</ref>

| Pediatrics
| [[Julie Mennella]] and [[Gary Beauchamp]]
| "for studying what a [[Breastfeeding|nursing baby]] experiences when the baby's mother eats [[garlic]]"
| <ref name="v783">{{cite journal | last1=Mennella | first1=J. A. | last2=Beauchamp | first2=G. K. | title=Maternal diet alters the sensory qualities of human milk and the nursling's behavior | journal=Pediatrics | volume=88 | issue=4 | date=1991 | issn=0031-4005 | pmid=1896276 | pages=737–744 }}</ref>

| Physics
| Giacomo Bartolucci, Daniel Maria Busiello, Matteo Ciarchi, Alberto Corticelli, Ivan Di Terlizzi, Fabrizio Olmeda, Davide Revignas, and Vincenzo Maria Schimmenti
| "for discoveries about the physics of [[pasta sauce]], especially the phase transition that can lead to clumping, which can be a cause of unpleasantness"
| <ref name="e522">{{cite journal | last1=Bartolucci | first1=G. | last2=Busiello | first2=D. M. | last3=Ciarchi | first3=M. | last4=Corticelli | first4=A. | last5=Di Terlizzi | first5=I. | last6=Olmeda | first6=F. | last7=Revignas | first7=D. | last8=Schimmenti | first8=V. M. | title=Phase behavior of Cacio e Pepe sauce | journal=Physics of Fluids | volume=37 | issue=4 | date=2025-04-01 | issn=1070-6631 | doi=10.1063/5.0255841 | article-number=044122 | doi-access=free | arxiv=2501.00536 | bibcode=2025PhFl...37d4122B }}</ref>
|-
| Psychology
| Marcin Zajenkowski and [[Gilles Gignac]]
| "for investigating what happens when you tell [[narcissists]] — or anyone else — that they are intelligent"
| <ref name="g541">{{cite journal | last1=Zajenkowski | first1=Marcin | last2=Gignac | first2=Gilles E. | title=Telling people they are intelligent correlates with the feeling of narcissistic uniqueness: The influence of IQ feedback on temporary state narcissism | journal=Intelligence | volume=89 | date=2021 | doi=10.1016/j.intell.2021.101595 | article-number=101595 | url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S0160289621000799 | access-date=2025-12-13}}</ref>
|}

== People who received multiple Ig Nobel Prizes ==
* [[Jacques Benveniste]], 1991 and 1998 Chemistry
* [[Joseph Keller]], 1999 and 2012 Physics
* [[Toshiyuki Nakagaki]] and [[Atsushi Tero]], 2008 Cognitive Science and 2010 Transportation Planning
* Alessandro Pluchino and Andrea Rapisarda, 2010 Management and 2022 Economics
* [[Alexander Lukashenko]], 2013 Peace and 2020 Medical Education
* [[David Hu (scientist)|David Hu]] and Patricia Yang, 2015 and 2019 Physics

== Ig Nobel Prize winners who also received the Nobel Prize ==
* [[Andre Geim]], 2000 Ig Nobel Prize in Physics and 2010 [[Nobel Prize in Physics]].<ref>[https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/physics/2010/summary/ "The Nobel Prize in Physics 2010"]. Retrieved 17 September 2022</ref>
